message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Nav-Gruppierung mit Dropdowns

## Context
Die Navigation hat 10 Einträge (11 mit Aktuelles). Das ist zu viel für eine Zeile. Lösung: Zwei Dropdown-Gruppen fassen die Homepage-Anker zusammen. Eigenständige Seiten bleiben Top-Level.

## Neue Nav-Struktur (6 sichtbare Items statt 11)

| Nav-Item | Typ | Inhalt |
|----------|-----|--------|
| **Verein** ▾ | Dropdown | Über uns, Videos, Vorstand, Ziele, Mitglied werden |
| **Funk** ▾ | Dropdown | Funkbetrieb, Krisenkommunikation |
| Aktuelles | Link | `/aktuelles/` |
| Termine | Link | `/termine/` |
| Presse | Link | `/presse/` |
| Kontakt | Link | `#kontakt` / `/#kontakt` |

## Technischer Ansatz

CSS-only Dropdowns auf Desktop (`:hover` öffnet Panel). Auf Mobile (≤768px) werden Sub-Items flach eingerückt angezeigt (immer sichtbar, kein Hover). Minimales JS nur für `aria-expanded` Accessibility + Touch-Tap-Support.

## Dateien (3 Änderungen)

### 1. `src/_includes/nav.njk`
Flache `<ul>` wird ersetzt durch Dropdown-Gruppen:
- `<li class="nav__dropdown">` mit `<button class="nav__dropdown-toggle">` + `<ul class="nav__dropdown-menu">`
- Verein-Dropdown: Über uns, Videos, Vorstand, Ziele, Mitglied werden
- Funk-Dropdown: Funkbetrieb, Krisenkommunikation
- Direkte Links: Aktuelles, Termine, Presse, Kontakt

### 2. `src/css/base.css`
Neue CSS-Regeln nach `.nav__links a.active`:

**Desktop:**
- `.nav__dropdown` — `position: relative`
- `.nav__dropdown-toggle` — Button gleich gestylt wie Nav-Links, mit ▾ Chevron
- `.nav__dropdown-menu` — `position: absolute; display: none; background: var(--polizei-dark); min-width: 200px; box-shadow; border`
- `.nav__dropdown:hover .nav__dropdown-menu` — `display: block`
- `.nav__dropdown-menu a` — `padding: 10px 20px; white-space: nowrap`

**Mobile (768px):**
- `.nav__dropdown-toggle` — sichtbarer Gruppen-Header
- `.nav__dropdown-menu` — `display: block; position: static; padding-left: 16px` (eingerückt, immer offen)

### 3. `src/_includes/base.njk`
Im bestehenden `<script>` Block: Dropdown-Toggles klicken → `aria-expanded` + `.open` Klasse togglen (Touch/Keyboard-Support). Hover funktioniert via CSS.

### 4. `src/_includes/footer.njk`
Aktuelles-Link wieder einfügen (Footer bleibt flach).

## Verifikation
```bash
nix-shell -p nodejs_20 --run "npx @11ty/eleventy"
```
- Nav zeigt 6 Items: Verein▾, Funk▾, Aktuelles, Termine, Presse, Kontakt
- Hover auf Desktop öffnet Dropdown-Panel
- Mobile: Sub-Items eingerückt sichtbar


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/achildrenmile/.claude/projects/-home-achildrenmile-bosarsa/85fab2cf-71cd-45ad-afa8-2a4557374558.jsonl

planContent:
# Plan: Nav-Gruppierung mit Dropdowns

## Context
Die Navigation hat 10 Einträge (11 mit Aktuelles). Das ist zu viel für eine Zeile. Lösung: Zwei Dropdown-Gruppen fassen die Homepage-Anker zusammen. Eigenständige Seiten bleiben Top-Level.

## Neue Nav-Struktur (6 sichtbare Items statt 11)

| Nav-Item | Typ | Inhalt |
|----------|-----|--------|
| **Verein** ▾ | Dropdown | Über uns, Videos, Vorstand, Ziele, Mitglied werden |
| **Funk** ▾ | Dropdown | Funkbetrieb, Krisenkommunikation |
| Aktuelles | Link | `/aktuelles/` |
| Termine | Link | `/termine/` |
| Presse | Link | `/presse/` |
| Kontakt | Link | `#kontakt` / `/#kontakt` |

## Technischer Ansatz

CSS-only Dropdowns auf Desktop (`:hover` öffnet Panel). Auf Mobile (≤768px) werden Sub-Items flach eingerückt angezeigt (immer sichtbar, kein Hover). Minimales JS nur für `aria-expanded` Accessibility + Touch-Tap-Support.

## Dateien (3 Änderungen)

### 1. `src/_includes/nav.njk`
Flache `<ul>` wird ersetzt durch Dropdown-Gruppen:
- `<li class="nav__dropdown">` mit `<button class="nav__dropdown-toggle">` + `<ul class="nav__dropdown-menu">`
- Verein-Dropdown: Über uns, Videos, Vorstand, Ziele, Mitglied werden
- Funk-Dropdown: Funkbetrieb, Krisenkommunikation
- Direkte Links: Aktuelles, Termine, Presse, Kontakt

### 2. `src/css/base.css`
Neue CSS-Regeln nach `.nav__links a.active`:

**Desktop:**
- `.nav__dropdown` — `position: relative`
- `.nav__dropdown-toggle` — Button gleich gestylt wie Nav-Links, mit ▾ Chevron
- `.nav__dropdown-menu` — `position: absolute; display: none; background: var(--polizei-dark); min-width: 200px; box-shadow; border`
- `.nav__dropdown:hover .nav__dropdown-menu` — `display: block`
- `.nav__dropdown-menu a` — `padding: 10px 20px; white-space: nowrap`

**Mobile (768px):**
- `.nav__dropdown-toggle` — sichtbarer Gruppen-Header
- `.nav__dropdown-menu` — `display: block; position: static; padding-left: 16px` (eingerückt, immer offen)

### 3. `src/_includes/base.njk`
Im bestehenden `<script>` Block: Dropdown-Toggles klicken → `aria-expanded` + `.open` Klasse togglen (Touch/Keyboard-Support). Hover funktioniert via CSS.

### 4. `src/_includes/footer.njk`
Aktuelles-Link wieder einfügen (Footer bleibt flach).

## Verifikation
```bash
nix-shell -p nodejs_20 --run "npx @11ty/eleventy"
```
- Nav zeigt 6 Items: Verein▾, Funk▾, Aktuelles, Termine, Presse, Kontakt
- Hover auf Desktop öffnet Dropdown-Panel
- Mobile: Sub-Items eingerückt sichtbar

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="de">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>{{ title }}</title>
  <meta name="description" content="{{ description }}">
  {% if keywords %}<meta name="keywords" content="{{ keywords }}">{% endif %}
  <meta name="author" content="BOS-ARSA">
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow">
  <link rel="canonical" href="{{ canonical }}">
  <!-- Open Graph -->
  <meta property="og:title" content="{{ title }}">
  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ ogDescription | default(description) }}">
  <meta property="og:type" content="website">
  <meta property="og:url" content="{{ canonical }}">
  <meta property="og:locale" content="de_AT">
  <meta property="og:site_name" content="BOS-ARSA">
  <meta property="og:image" content="{{ site.url }}/og-image.png">
  <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200">
  <meta property="og:image:height" content="630">
  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="BOS-ARSA — Amateurfunk im Sinne der Sicherheit">
  <!-- Twitter Card -->
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
  <meta name="twitter:title" content="{{ title }}">
  <meta name="twitter:description" content="{{ ogDescription | default(description) }}">
  <meta name="twitter:image" content="{{ site.url }}/og-image.png">
  {% if structuredData %}
  <!-- Structured Data -->
  <script type="application/ld+json">
  {{ structuredData | dump | safe }}
  </script>
  {% endif %}
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="/favicon-96x96.png" sizes="96x96">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg">
  <link rel="shortcut icon" href="/favicon.ico">
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" sizes="180x180" href="/apple-touch-icon.png">
  <link rel="manifest" href="/site.webmanifest">
  <script src="/js/lucide.min.js"></script>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/base.css">
  {% if pageCSSFile %}<link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ pageCSSFile }}">{% endif %}
</head>
<body>

  {% include "nav.njk" %}

  {{ content | safe }}

  {% include "footer.njk" %}

  <script>
    lucide.createIcons();
    document.getElementById('year').textContent = new Date().getFullYear();

    // Nav scroll shadow
    const nav = document.getElementById('nav');
    window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
      nav.classList.toggle('scrolled', window.scrollY > 20);
    }, { passive: true });

    // Mobile menu
    const navToggle = document.getElementById('navToggle');
    const navLinks = document.getElementById('navLinks');
    navToggle.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const isOpen = navLinks.classList.toggle('open');
      navToggle.classList.toggle('active');
      navToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', isOpen);
      navToggle.setAttribute('aria-label', isOpen ? 'Menü schließen' : 'Menü öffnen');
      document.body.style.overflow = isOpen ? 'hidden' : '';
    });
    navLinks.querySelectorAll('a').forEach(link => {
      link.addEventListener('click', () => {
        navLinks.classList.remove('open');
        navToggle.classList.remove('active');
        navToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
        document.body.style.overflow = '';
      });
    });

    // Scroll reveal
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      entries.forEach(e => {
        if (e.isIntersecting) { e.target.classList.add('visible'); observer.unobserve(e.target); }
      });
    }, { threshold: 0.1, rootMargin: '0px 0px -30px 0px' });
    document.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));

    // Stagger children
    document.querySelectorAll('.features, .board-grid').forEach(grid => {
      grid.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach((c, i) => { c.style.transitionDelay = `${i * .08}s`; });
    });
  </script>
</body>
</html>

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of the changes:

### `src/_includes/nav.njk`
- Replaced flat 10-item `<ul>` with **6 visible items**: 2 dropdown groups + 4 direct links
- **Verein** dropdown: Über uns, Videos, Vorstand, Ziele, Mitglied werden
- **Funk** dropdown: Funkbetrieb, Krisenkommunikation
- Direct links: Aktuelles, Termine, Presse, Kontakt

### `src/css/base.css`
- Added dropdown styles: `.nav__dropdown`, `.nav__dropdown-toggle`, `.nav__dropdown-menu`, `.nav__chevron`
- **Desktop**: CSS-only `:hover` opens dropdown panel (absolute positioned, dark navy background, box-shadow)
- **Mobile (≤768px)**: Sub-items are always visible, flat/indented with `padding-left: 16px`, group headers styled as gold labels, chevron hidden

### `src/_includes/base.njk`
- Added JS for **touch/keyboard support**: clicking toggle buttons opens/closes dropdowns with `aria-expanded` + `.open` class
- Closes other dropdowns when opening one; closes all on outside click

### `src/_includes/footer.njk`
- Added Aktuelles link to footer navigation list

# Navigation Structure Analysis

Based on the webpage content provided, I cannot show you the complete HTML of the nav element because **the actual HTML markup is not included in the provided content**. The page source shows only:

1. **JavaScript that manipulates the nav** - event listeners for mobile menu toggles, dropdown functionality, and accessibility attributes
2. **References to nav elements** via IDs and classes like `nav__dropdown-toggle`, `nav__dropdown`, `navToggle`, `navLinks`

## What I Can See:

The navigation contains these sections:
- "Verein" dropdown (About us, Videos, Board, Goals, Membership)
- "Funk" dropdown (Radio operation, Crisis communication)
- News, Events, Press, Contact links

## Potential Issues Identified:

1. **Mobile menu overflow** - Script sets `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'` when menu opens, which could cause layout shift
2. **Dropdown management** - Only one dropdown closes others, but nested dropdowns might have focus issues
3. **Touch accessibility** - Relies on click events; touch devices need proper testing
4. **No visible CSS** - Styling isn't provided, so visual glitches can't be assessed

**To properly debug**, you'd need the actual HTML markup and CSS stylesheet, which aren't included in this content extraction.

        navLinks.classList.remove('open');
        navToggle.classList.remove('active');

        document.body.style.overflow = '';

input.prompt: Show the complete CSS for nav, dropdown, and responsive media query sections. Look for any CSS issues like missing list-style:none on dropdown menus, specificity conflicts, or layout problems.

content.content:
# CSS Analysis: Nav, Dropdown & Responsive Sections

## Complete CSS Code

The navigation system comprises three main sections:

**Primary Navigation Styling:**
The `.nav` uses fixed positioning with a dark police-blue background. The brand section combines a logo (44px height) with text styling using the gold accent color. Navigation links employ a 0.82rem font size with hover states adding a semi-transparent white background.

**Dropdown Implementation:**
The `.nav__dropdown-menu` is positioned absolutely below its toggle with `display: none` as default. It shows on hover or when the parent has the `open` class. The menu features a navy background with subtle borders and box-shadow for depth. Links within maintain consistent 0.82rem sizing with the same hover/active color states.

**Responsive Adjustments (768px and below):**
Mobile view converts the horizontal nav links into a fixed, off-canvas sidebar. The `.nav__links` slides in from the right (using `translateX(100%)` → `translateX(0)`). The hamburger menu button activates this mobile nav. Dropdown menus become static, full-width elements nested under their toggles. The brand subtitle hides on mobile.

## Issues Identified

1. **No specificity conflicts detected** — selectors maintain consistent precedence
2. **List styling is correct** — `.nav__links` and `.nav__dropdown-menu` both declare `list-style: none`
3. **Layout concern:** Mobile dropdown menus lack explicit `max-height` or scroll containment, potentially causing layout overflow if menus exceed viewport height
4. **Minor gap:** The mobile nav doesn't specify a `max-height`, which could allow the menu to extend beyond the visible area

# CSS Analysis: Nav, Dropdown & Responsive Sections

## Complete CSS Code

The navigation system comprises three main sections:

**Primary Navigation Styling:**
The `.nav` uses fixed positioning with a dark police-blue background. The brand section combines a logo (44px height) with text styling using the gold accent color. Navigation links employ a 0.82rem font size with hover states adding a semi-transparent white background.

**Dropdown Implementation:**
The `.nav__dropdown-menu` is positioned absolutely below its toggle with `display: none` as default. It shows on hover or when the parent has the `open` class. The menu features a navy background with subtle borders and box-shadow for depth. Links within maintain consistent 0.82rem sizing with the same hover/active color states.

**Responsive Adjustments (768px and below):**
Mobile view converts the horizontal nav links into a fixed, off-canvas sidebar. The `.nav__links` slides in from the right (using `translateX(100%)` → `translateX(0)`). The hamburger menu button activates this mobile nav. Dropdown menus become static, full-width elements nested under their toggles. The brand subtitle hides on mobile.

## Issues Identified

1. **No specificity conflicts detected** — selectors maintain consistent precedence
2. **List styling is correct** — `.nav__links` and `.nav__dropdown-menu` both declare `list-style: none`
3. **Layout concern:** Mobile dropdown menus lack explicit `max-height` or scroll containment, potentially causing layout overflow if menus exceed viewport height
4. **Minor gap:** The mobile nav doesn't specify a `max-height`, which could allow the menu to extend beyond the visible area

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

# Load environment variables
if [ -f ".env.production" ]; then
    source .env.production
else
    echo -e "${RED}Error: .env.production file not found${NC}"
    exit 1
fi

# Validate required variables
REQUIRED_VARS="DEPLOY_HOST REMOTE_DIR CONTAINER_NAME IMAGE_NAME CONTAINER_PORT SITE_URL"
for var in $REQUIRED_VARS; do
    if [ -z "${!var}" ]; then
        echo -e "${RED}Error: $var is not set in .env.production${NC}"
        exit 1
    fi
done

# Check for --rebuild flag
REBUILD_FLAG=""
if [ "$1" == "--rebuild" ]; then
    REBUILD_FLAG="--no-cache"
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Rebuild flag set — will build without cache${NC}"
fi

echo -e "${GREEN}=== Deploying BOS-ARSA to $DEPLOY_HOST ===${NC}"
echo "Host: $DEPLOY_HOST"
echo "Remote dir: $REMOTE_DIR"
echo "Container: $CONTAINER_NAME"
echo "Port: $CONTAINER_PORT"
echo ""

# Step 1: Ensure remote directory exists and sync files
echo -e "${GREEN}Step 1: Syncing files...${NC}"
ssh "$DEPLOY_HOST" "mkdir -p '$REMOTE_DIR'"
rsync -avz --delete \
    --exclude '.git' \
    --exclude '.env.production' \
    ./ "$DEPLOY_HOST:$REMOTE_DIR/"

# Step 2: Build and start container
echo -e "${GREEN}Step 2: Building and starting container...${NC}"
ssh "$DEPLOY_HOST" "
    cd '$REMOTE_DIR'
    docker build $REBUILD_FLAG -t '$IMAGE_NAME' .
    docker compose up -d --force-recreate --build
"

# Step 3: Verify deployment
echo -e "${GREEN}Step 3: Verifying deployment...${NC}"
sleep 3

CONTAINER_STATUS=$(ssh "$DEPLOY_HOST" "docker ps --filter 'name=$CONTAINER_NAME' --format '{{.Status}}'")
echo "Container status: $CONTAINER_STATUS"

LOCAL_PORT=$(echo $CONTAINER_PORT | cut -d: -f1)
if ssh "$DEPLOY_HOST" "curl -sf -o /dev/null http://localhost:$LOCAL_PORT"; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Container is running on port $LOCAL_PORT${NC}"
else
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ Container not yet responding on port $LOCAL_PORT${NC}"
fi

if curl -sf -o /dev/null "$SITE_URL" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ Site is accessible at $SITE_URL${NC}"
else
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ Public URL not yet accessible — configure Cloudflare tunnel${NC}"
fi

echo ""
echo -e "${GREEN}=== Deployment complete ===${NC}"

content.thinking:
Looking at the output, the compose build STILL has cached COPY and eleventy steps:```
#10 [build 5/6] COPY . .#10 CACHED
#11 [build 6/6] RUN npx @11ty/eleventy
#11 CACHED
```The `--no-cache` flag was only applied to `docker build`, not to `docker compose up --build`. The compose build still uses its own cache.

The deploy script does:```bash
docker build $REBUILD_FLAG -t '$IMAGE_NAME' .docker compose up -d --force-recreate --build```

`$REBUILD_FLAG` is `--no-cache` and it's only passed to `docker build`, not to `docker compose`. The compose build caches from its own build cache.So even with `--rebuild`, the compose build still uses cached layers. The `--no-cache` flag only affects the standalone `docker build` command.I need to also pass `--no-cache` to the compose build. But I shouldn't modify the deploy script without permission.

I should try running the compose build with `--no-cache` directly via SSH. In Docker Compose v2, I can pass `--no-cache` to `docker compose build`, then follow it with `docker compose up -d --force-recreate` to apply the changes.

But looking at the build output, the `COPY . .` step is cached even though we rsynced new CSS files. Docker BuildKit uses content-based checksums for the build context, so it's not detecting the file changes—I need to figure out why the checksums aren't reflecting the updated files.

content.text: The compose build is still caching. The `--no-cache` only applies to `docker build`, not `docker compose`. Let me force the compose build cache to clear.

input.command: ssh achildrenmile@host-node-01 "cd /home/achildrenmile/apps/bosarsa && docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d --force-recreate" 2>&1
input.description: Force compose rebuild without cache on remote

Der Fix: `docker compose build --no-cache` hat einen sauberen Build erzwungen. Die Nav-Dropdowns sollten jetzt auf Desktop (Hover) und Mobile (immer sichtbar, eingerückt) korrekt funktionieren.

content.thinking:
The user says the Desktop Dropdown is broken. Let me analyze what could be wrong with the desktop dropdown. Let me check the deployed site more carefully.

The issues I can think of for desktop dropdowns:

1. The dropdown menu doesn't appear on hover
2. The dropdown menu appears but immediately disappears when trying to move to it
3. The dropdown menu appears behind the page content (z-index issue)
4. The dropdown toggle buttons look different from regular nav links
5. The dropdown menu positioning is wrong

Let me fetch the page and look at the CSS more carefully. Also, let me think about hover behavior.

One thing I know is that the dropdown is positioned with `top: 100%` relative to the `<li class="nav__dropdown">`. 

The `<li>` height is determined by the button content, and since it's vertically centered in the fixed 72px nav, the dropdown menu positioned at `top: 100%` should start right at the bottom of the `<li>`. Since the dropdown is a child of the `<li>`, hovering over it should maintain the hover state, so there shouldn't be a gap issue. But I'm wondering if there's something else going on with how the `<li>` is being sized or positioned. The dropdown sits flush against the button with no gap, so hover state should persist as the user moves between them. I'm now considering other potential issues—maybe the dropdown isn't displaying due to CSS specificity, or there's a visual rendering problem. Looking at the z-index setup, the dropdown has z-index 1001 while the nav itself is 1000, but since the dropdown is a child of the nav's fixed positioning context, it should layer correctly above the page content below. The dropdown starts about 1px below the nav bar and should overlay on top of the hero section since the nav has a high z-index. But maybe the issue is that the `:hover` state on the dropdown isn't triggering properly in the first place.

Actually, I'm second-guessing myself on how `:hover` works with nested elements. The button is inside the `<li>`, so hovering over the button should still activate the parent's `:hover` state. But I'm wondering if there's something about the button element itself that's interfering with the hover behavior.

Let me consider whether the dropdown is actually appearing but just invisible. Looking at the styling, the dropdown has a dark navy background, a subtle white border, and a shadow effect, with light gray text for the links. That should definitely be visible against most backgrounds.

Now I'm checking the positioning setup for the dropdown menu...

The dropdown sits absolutely positioned below the button with `top: 100%; left: 0`, which should place it correctly. With longer menu items like "Mitglied werden" and the `min-width: 200px` constraint, the menu might extend wider than expected, but that shouldn't prevent it from showing.

I'm wondering if the real issue is that the dropdown button itself doesn't clearly signal it's interactive. The button uses the same styling as regular nav links with just a tiny chevron indicator, which might not be obvious enough to users that there's a dropdown menu available.

But the user said the dropdown is "broken," which suggests a functional problem rather than a visibility issue. I'm thinking the interaction model might be the culprit—if the dropdown opens on hover and the button click also toggles an `.open` class, then clicking the button keeps the dropdown visible even after the mouse leaves, since the `.open` state persists until an outside-click handler removes it. This could create confusing behavior where the dropdown doesn't close when expected. I'm realizing the core issue: on touch devices, `:hover` persists after the first tap, so toggling `.open` off doesn't actually close the dropdown because the hover state keeps it visible. The user mentioned the dropdown doesn't work on desktop, which suggests I should verify the actual CSS behavior in a browser rather than continuing to theorize about the interaction model. I'm wondering if the dropdown menu is actually appearing but positioned off-screen or hidden behind other elements—maybe the positioning relative to the `<li>` element isn't accounting for the nav's position on the page, or the menu gets cut off by the viewport when the nav items are compressed on smaller screens. the dropdown functionality with JavaScript that toggles an open state and manages which dropdowns are visible at any given time.

I'm checking if there's a JavaScript error in this code—specifically whether `forEach()` works properly on the NodeList returned by `querySelectorAll()`, since that could be preventing the entire script from executing and breaking the navigation.

Actually, modern browsers support `forEach()` on NodeLists and arrow functions, so that's not the issue. The dropdown toggle logic and outside click handler look fine too. But I'm realizing the real culprit might be earlier in the script—if `lucide.createIcons()` fails to load or throws an error, it would halt execution of everything that comes after it, including all the navigation code.

Though if that were the case, the mobile hamburger menu wouldn't work either, and the user only mentioned the desktop dropdown being broken. Plus, the CSS hover state should still function even without JavaScript, so the dropdown would at least appear on hover for mouse users.

Let me step back and think about what actually changed. Before, the nav was just a flat list of ten links with no dropdowns at all. Now I've restructured it with nested items and dropdown functionality. That's the key difference—something in how I've organized the HTML or CSS for the dropdown structure is likely causing the issue.

Actually, I'm wondering if the dropdown menu is being hidden behind something else on the page. Let me check the z-index values. The nav is fixed with a z-index of 1000, which should keep it on top, and the hero section only has z-index values of 1 and 2, so that shouldn't be the problem. The dropdown should be visible since it's nested inside the nav.

Wait, I need to think about whether the nav's height is clipping the dropdown content that extends below it.

Actually, let me step back and test this more directly—I should create a minimal reproduction case to see if the hover state is even triggering properly. Or maybe the issue is something else entirely, like a `.dockerignore` file that's preventing CSS files from being included in the build.

`cache-control: public, max-age=604800` — The CSS is cached for 604800 seconds (7 DAYS!)
`content-length: 13918` — The cached file is 13918 bytes

But the CURRENT CSS file should be larger (it has the dropdown styles). Let me check what the current CSS file size is locally.

Wait, but earlier I already checked and the curl output DID contain the dropdown styles (when I did `grep "nav__dropdown"`). 

So the dropdown styles are definitely in the CSS being served, but Cloudflare's cache is stale — it's holding onto an older version from 54 minutes ago while the actual file on the server has been updated. That's why the browser is getting the old cached version without the dropdown styles. The `last-modified` and `age` headers tell me the file was last updated at 19:20:50 UTC and has been cached for about 54 minutes, which means the CDN has the older version from before the latest deploy. But since the current deployed CSS actually includes the dropdown styles, the real issue is likely the user's browser cache—with a 7-day max-age directive, their browser is serving stale CSS without even checking the server for updates.

The solution is to add a cache-busting version parameter to the CSS URL so the browser treats it as a new resource and fetches the updated styles, then redeploy the HTML to reference that new URL. I should also check the nginx configuration to see what cache headers are being set, since that's really where the issue originates.

content.text:
Found it! Cloudflare is caching the CSS for 7 days (`cache-control: public, max-age=604800`). The user's browser has the **old CSS** cached (before dropdown styles). The HTML has new dropdown markup, but the browser serves stale CSS.

Fix: add cache-busting parameter to the CSS URL.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="de">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>{{ title }}</title>
  <meta name="description" content="{{ description }}">
  {% if keywords %}<meta name="keywords" content="{{ keywords }}">{% endif %}
  <meta name="author" content="BOS-ARSA">
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow">
  <link rel="canonical" href="{{ canonical }}">
  <!-- Open Graph -->
  <meta property="og:title" content="{{ title }}">
  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ ogDescription | default(description) }}">
  <meta property="og:type" content="website">
  <meta property="og:url" content="{{ canonical }}">
  <meta property="og:locale" content="de_AT">
  <meta property="og:site_name" content="BOS-ARSA">
  <meta property="og:image" content="{{ site.url }}/og-image.png">
  <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200">
  <meta property="og:image:height" content="630">
  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="BOS-ARSA — Amateurfunk im Sinne der Sicherheit">
  <!-- Twitter Card -->
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
  <meta name="twitter:title" content="{{ title }}">
  <meta name="twitter:description" content="{{ ogDescription | default(description) }}">
  <meta name="twitter:image" content="{{ site.url }}/og-image.png">
  {% if structuredData %}
  <!-- Structured Data -->
  <script type="application/ld+json">
  {{ structuredData | dump | safe }}
  </script>
  {% endif %}
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="/favicon-96x96.png" sizes="96x96">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg">
  <link rel="shortcut icon" href="/favicon.ico">
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" sizes="180x180" href="/apple-touch-icon.png">
  <link rel="manifest" href="/site.webmanifest">
  <script src="/js/lucide.min.js"></script>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/base.css">
  {% if pageCSSFile %}<link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ pageCSSFile }}">{% endif %}
</head>
<body>

  {% include "nav.njk" %}

  {{ content | safe }}

  {% include "footer.njk" %}

  <script>
    lucide.createIcons();
    document.getElementById('year').textContent = new Date().getFullYear();

    // Nav scroll shadow
    const nav = document.getElementById('nav');
    window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
      nav.classList.toggle('scrolled', window.scrollY > 20);
    }, { passive: true });

    // Mobile menu
    const navToggle = document.getElementById('navToggle');
    const navLinks = document.getElementById('navLinks');
    navToggle.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const isOpen = navLinks.classList.toggle('open');
      navToggle.classList.toggle('active');
      navToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', isOpen);
      navToggle.setAttribute('aria-label', isOpen ? 'Menü schließen' : 'Menü öffnen');
      document.body.style.overflow = isOpen ? 'hidden' : '';
    });
    navLinks.querySelectorAll('a').forEach(link => {
      link.addEventListener('click', () => {
        navLinks.classList.remove('open');
        navToggle.classList.remove('active');
        navToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
        document.body.style.overflow = '';
      });
    });

    // Dropdown toggles (touch + keyboard)
    document.querySelectorAll('.nav__dropdown-toggle').forEach(btn => {
      btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        const dd = btn.parentElement;
        const isOpen = dd.classList.toggle('open');
        btn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', isOpen);
        // close other dropdowns
        document.querySelectorAll('.nav__dropdown').forEach(other => {
          if (other !== dd) {
            other.classList.remove('open');
            other.querySelector('.nav__dropdown-toggle').setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
          }
        });
      });
    });
    // Close dropdowns on outside click
    document.addEventListener('click', e => {
      if (!e.target.closest('.nav__dropdown')) {
        document.querySelectorAll('.nav__dropdown').forEach(dd => {
          dd.classList.remove('open');
          dd.querySelector('.nav__dropdown-toggle').setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
        });
      }
    });

    // Scroll reveal
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      entries.forEach(e => {
        if (e.isIntersecting) { e.target.classList.add('visible'); observer.unobserve(e.target); }
      });
    }, { threshold: 0.1, rootMargin: '0px 0px -30px 0px' });
    document.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));

    // Stagger children
    document.querySelectorAll('.features, .board-grid').forEach(grid => {
      grid.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach((c, i) => { c.style.transitionDelay = `${i * .08}s`; });
    });
  </script>
</body>
</html>

content.text: Now let me also check and fix the nginx cache config so this doesn't happen again.

content.thinking:
The nginx config caches CSS for 1 week (604800 seconds) without any revalidation. This is why the browser caches the old CSS. 

The `?v=2` query parameter trick will force browsers to fetch the new CSS because the URL is different. But this is a manual process - we'd need to update the version every time the CSS changes.

A better approach would be to either:
1. Use shorter cache times with `must-revalidate` for CSS
2. Use ETags properly (nginx does this by default if `etag on`)

But for now, the `?v=2` fix is the most immediate solution. Let me deploy and verify.


I can optimize the nginx caching strategy later, but right now I need to get the menu working for the user. Let me rebuild and deploy.

content.text: CSS is cached 1 week with no revalidation - that's the root cause. The `?v=2` cache-buster will fix this immediately. Let me build and deploy.

content.thinking:
The compose build still cached. But this time it doesn't matter because the IMPORTANT change is in the HTML (`base.njk`), not the CSS. The CSS URL in the HTML now has `?v=2`, which will force browsers to fetch the new CSS regardless of their cache.But wait, the compose build CACHED the COPY step again. This means the compose-built image might have the OLD `base.njk` without `?v=2`. Let me check.Hmm, but the first `docker build --no-cache` ran fresh (steps 13-15 were NOT cached). And then the compose build used CACHED layers. The container uses the compose-built image.


So the compose image might NOT have `?v=2` in the HTML! Looking at the deploy output more carefully, the first build ran the COPY and build steps fresh without caching, but the compose build reused those cached layers entirely. That means the deployed container is running the old HTML without the cache-busting query parameter.

The `--no-cache` flag I used only applied to the first docker build command, not to docker compose. I need to run `docker compose build --no-cache` on the remote to force a fresh build.

content.text: The compose build is still caching. Let me force it properly.

input.command: ssh achildrenmile@host-node-01 "cd /home/achildrenmile/apps/bosarsa && docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d --force-recreate" 2>&1
input.description: Force compose rebuild without cache

toolUseResult.stdout: <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/base.css?v=2">

content.text:
Die HTML-Seite referenziert jetzt `/css/base.css?v=2`. Damit wird der Browser-Cache und Cloudflare-Cache umgangen und die neue CSS-Datei mit allen Dropdown-Styles geladen.

**Bitte lade die Seite neu** (evtl. mit Ctrl+Shift+R / Hard Reload). Das Problem war:
- nginx sendet `Cache-Control: max-age=604800` (7 Tage) für CSS-Dateien
- Dein Browser und Cloudflare haben die alte CSS-Datei (ohne Dropdown-Styles) gecacht
- Die neue HTML-Struktur (mit Dropdowns) traf auf alte CSS-Regeln = kaputt
- `?v=2` erzwingt einen frischen Download der CSS-Datei

message.content: kontakt scrolls a bit too much

content.thinking:
The user says that clicking "Kontakt" scrolls too much - meaning it scrolls past the contact section. The Kontakt link points to `#kontakt` which is the footer element (`<footer class="footer" id="kontakt">`). The issue is likely that `scroll-padding-top: var(--nav-h)` (72px) pushes the view too far, or the footer is at the very bottom of the page and the browser can't scroll far enough, or it scrolls TOO far past the target.

Actually, "scrolls a bit too much" means the page scrolls further than it should - the contact section ends up too high, with too much empty space below, or the heading is hidden behind the nav. Wait, let me re-read: "scrolls a bit too much" - this means when clicking Kontakt, the page scrolls DOWN too much, so the contact area is pushed up too high on screen, or the viewport goes past it.


Since the footer sits at the very bottom of the page, clicking the anchor tries to align its top with the scroll-padding offset, but the browser can only scroll to the page's end. This means the footer ends up at the bottom of the viewport rather than at the top, so "scrolls too much" might actually mean the scroll-padding-top is creating unwanted offset or the anchor target itself is misaligned. I'm realizing the issue might be the opposite—the anchor scrolls past the target, showing too much of the page below instead of keeping the contact section visible. Since the `#kontakt` ID is on the footer element itself, the browser scrolls to align it with the `scroll-padding-top` offset, but with the footer's top padding already in place, the content above it gets pushed completely out of view. I think the issue is that the page scrolls too far down, leaving the footer content awkwardly positioned in the middle of the screen with empty space below. The fix would be to add `scroll-margin-top` to the footer element to reduce how much it scrolls—more scroll margin means it stops scrolling earlier. Let me check what adjustment the footer needs. saying the footer doesn't scroll into view properly when clicking the Kontakt link, likely because the scroll offset isn't accounting for the navigation height correctly or the page can't scroll far enough to position the footer at the desired viewport location.

I think the real issue is that the footer has padding above its content, so even when the footer element scrolls into view at 72px from the top, the actual contact information sits much lower due to that padding. I should add `scroll-margin-top` to adjust where the scroll stops, or reduce the footer's top padding so the content appears closer to where the user expects it. scroll-margin-top: 20px` would actually scroll further down, placing the footer at 92px from the viewport top instead of less. I need to use a negative value to reduce scrolling and keep more content visible above the footer.

Since the user mentioned scrolling "a bit" too much, I'll try a modest adjustment like `scroll-margin-top: 24px` or `32px`. The footer's padding and current scroll-padding-top positioning means the content starts quite far down the viewport, so I should test what feels right.

content.text: The footer has `padding: 56px 0 0` and the scroll-padding accounts for the 72px nav. Adding `scroll-margin-top` to the footer will reduce how far it scrolls.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="de">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>{{ title }}</title>
  <meta name="description" content="{{ description }}">
  {% if keywords %}<meta name="keywords" content="{{ keywords }}">{% endif %}
  <meta name="author" content="BOS-ARSA">
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow">
  <link rel="canonical" href="{{ canonical }}">
  <!-- Open Graph -->
  <meta property="og:title" content="{{ title }}">
  <meta property="og:description" content="{{ ogDescription | default(description) }}">
  <meta property="og:type" content="website">
  <meta property="og:url" content="{{ canonical }}">
  <meta property="og:locale" content="de_AT">
  <meta property="og:site_name" content="BOS-ARSA">
  <meta property="og:image" content="{{ site.url }}/og-image.png">
  <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200">
  <meta property="og:image:height" content="630">
  <meta property="og:image:alt" content="BOS-ARSA — Amateurfunk im Sinne der Sicherheit">
  <!-- Twitter Card -->
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
  <meta name="twitter:title" content="{{ title }}">
  <meta name="twitter:description" content="{{ ogDescription | default(description) }}">
  <meta name="twitter:image" content="{{ site.url }}/og-image.png">
  {% if structuredData %}
  <!-- Structured Data -->
  <script type="application/ld+json">
  {{ structuredData | dump | safe }}
  </script>
  {% endif %}
  <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="/favicon-96x96.png" sizes="96x96">
  <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg">
  <link rel="shortcut icon" href="/favicon.ico">
  <link rel="apple-touch-icon" sizes="180x180" href="/apple-touch-icon.png">
  <link rel="manifest" href="/site.webmanifest">
  <script src="/js/lucide.min.js"></script>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/base.css?v=2">
  {% if pageCSSFile %}<link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ pageCSSFile }}">{% endif %}
</head>
<body>

  {% include "nav.njk" %}

  {{ content | safe }}

  {% include "footer.njk" %}

  <script>
    lucide.createIcons();
    document.getElementById('year').textContent = new Date().getFullYear();

    // Nav scroll shadow
    const nav = document.getElementById('nav');
    window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
      nav.classList.toggle('scrolled', window.scrollY > 20);
    }, { passive: true });

    // Mobile menu
    const navToggle = document.getElementById('navToggle');
    const navLinks = document.getElementById('navLinks');
    navToggle.addEventListener('click', () => {
      const isOpen = navLinks.classList.toggle('open');
      navToggle.classList.toggle('active');
      navToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', isOpen);
      navToggle.setAttribute('aria-label', isOpen ? 'Menü schließen' : 'Menü öffnen');
      document.body.style.overflow = isOpen ? 'hidden' : '';
    });
    navLinks.querySelectorAll('a').forEach(link => {
      link.addEventListener('click', () => {
        navLinks.classList.remove('open');
        navToggle.classList.remove('active');
        navToggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
        document.body.style.overflow = '';
      });
    });

    // Dropdown toggles (touch + keyboard)
    document.querySelectorAll('.nav__dropdown-toggle').forEach(btn => {
      btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        const dd = btn.parentElement;
        const isOpen = dd.classList.toggle('open');
        btn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', isOpen);
        // close other dropdowns
        document.querySelectorAll('.nav__dropdown').forEach(other => {
          if (other !== dd) {
            other.classList.remove('open');
            other.querySelector('.nav__dropdown-toggle').setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
          }
        });
      });
    });
    // Close dropdowns on outside click
    document.addEventListener('click', e => {
      if (!e.target.closest('.nav__dropdown')) {
        document.querySelectorAll('.nav__dropdown').forEach(dd => {
          dd.classList.remove('open');
          dd.querySelector('.nav__dropdown-toggle').setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
        });
      }
    });

    // Scroll reveal
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      entries.forEach(e => {
        if (e.isIntersecting) { e.target.classList.add('visible'); observer.unobserve(e.target); }
      });
    }, { threshold: 0.1, rootMargin: '0px 0px -30px 0px' });
    document.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));

    // Stagger children
    document.querySelectorAll('.features, .board-grid').forEach(grid => {
      grid.querySelectorAll('.reveal').forEach((c, i) => { c.style.transitionDelay = `${i * .08}s`; });
    });
  </script>
</body>
</html>

content.thinking:
The compose build still cached the COPY and eleventy steps. But the first `docker build` ran fresh (steps 11-12 were NOT cached). The issue is the compose build has its own cache.

But wait - the first build ran fresh and created `bosarsa:latest` with the new files. The compose build created `bosarsa-bosarsa:latest` with cached (old) files. The container uses the compose image.

I need to also do `docker compose build --no-cache` to make the compose build use fresh files. Let me do that.

content.text: Compose hat wieder gecacht. Muss den Compose-Build separat erzwingen.

input.command: ssh achildrenmile@host-node-01 "cd /home/achildrenmile/apps/bosarsa && docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d --force-recreate" 2>&1

content.text:
Deployed. Der Footer hat jetzt `scroll-margin-top: 24px` - das Kontakt-Scrollen sollte jetzt weniger weit gehen. CSS ist auf `?v=3` gebumpt.

Ich sollte auch das Deploy-Script fixen, damit `docker compose` künftig nicht mehr cached. Soll ich das machen?
